Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

I think the scope of this patch is quite a bit different from what I imagined and described in the ticket?

I was really hoping we can do the tracking at all times, and it's the crashing part that would be made optional.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, approved

(1 inline comment)

Shall we add an environment variable in t-f to run acc-sm with this declare tracking enabled?

Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

Oleg: yes I need to agree that I better followed LU-3110 title than your comment on what should be done ... Sorry.
I can revert my patch to strictly follow your needs or may be add the dynamic LBUG/Warning choice mechanism ?

Johann: I don't know the process used in the tests to integrate such parameters, where can I find this ? Or may be you can point me on an equivalent I can mimic ?

Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

I don't think we need a module parameter, but the LASSERT() needs to be removed entirely. 

As for environment variables, it would be enough to check something like:

 check_and_setup_lustre() {
        :
        :
        if [ -n "$OSD_TRACK_DECLARES" ]; then
                $LCTL set_param osd-*.*.track_declares=1 2> /dev/null
        fi

Patch Set 7: Looks good to me, approved

Some minor notes:
- if one part of an if/else needs braces {} then the other part should use them also
- I think it makes sense to set OSD_TRACK_DECLARES_LBUG by default in the test-framework.sh init_test_env() so that this is always being tested. Otherwise we will not see the error messages being printed during tests. This will not affect real world usage.

This is a minor issue that can be fixed in a separate patch, and is not needed for 2.4.0 like the test of this patch is.

Patch Set 7: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(3 inline comments)

Patch Set 7:

One other issue that should be fixed in the follow-on patch is to make the track_declares_assert tunable only be set for osd-ldiskfs, and not osd-*, since this functionality is not present in osd-zfs, and this would cause spurious warnings on each mount.
Patch Set 7: Looks good to me, approved

parents.subject: LU-3124 llite: To use an extra RPC to transfer layout

commit.subject: LU-3110 osd-ldiskfs: Dynamic LBUG in osd declares tracking
commit.message:
LU-3110 osd-ldiskfs: Dynamic LBUG in osd declares tracking

This patch implements a dynamic way to enable/disable osd
declaration tracking LBUGs.

OSD_TRACK_DECLARES define usage has been removed, and
tracking of declares is no longer a compile time option.

Enable/disable of declares tracking LBUGs is done via new
global lprocfs "track_declares_assert" boolean, also a
module-parameter.

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: I5164c51c3c3362a77d1a0c7cb7b9f63383b00403
